name: Moth

attacks.name: Divebomb Headbutt
attacks.tell: It drops from the air in a direct swoop to slam you with its head.
attacks.howToAvoid: Dodge to one side when the dive closes; its line is fairly straight.
attacks.notes: This is its strongest physical punish and its main option once it commits to fighting.

attacks.name: Dust Gust
attacks.tell: It beats its wings and throws a wave of dust forward.
attacks.howToAvoid: Keep moving sideways and do not let the dust cut off your path.
attacks.notes: The gust applies bleed and can also slow your attack tempo.

attacks.name: Dust Cloud
attacks.tell: It suspends dust over a broad zone, similar to bee pollen but much more dangerous.
attacks.howToAvoid: Leave the area before you get trapped inside the lingering effect.
attacks.notes: The cloud inflicts bleed, lowers movement speed, and also reduces attack speed.

description: A nocturnal creature that usually rests in the high areas of the rotten stump and upper grasslands. When it decides to fight, it fills the field with bleeding dust.
behavior: It is extremely lazy and spends most of its time asleep, even at night. When it wakes, it rarely looks for trouble on its own and prefers to glide or crawl lazily before perching again.
interactionWithPlayer: It stays neutral toward the player until attacked. Once engaged, it alternates between divebombs and ranged dust to punish anyone fighting it in the open.
interactionWithCreatures: It usually does not interact with other creatures and tends to sleep far from them in high or awkward positions.
strategy: Do not underestimate it just because it is passive. You need to manage distance, punish the divebomb when it comes down, and leave dust clouds before bleed and slows start stacking up.

combatStats.attackDamageSummary:
Divebomb Headbutt: 150
Dust Gust: 100
Dust Cloud: 75

specialTraits: It is immune to dust. Its clouds reduce movement speed and attack speed in addition to inflicting bleed.
